title: Cluster Setup and Management
description: Initialize and configure a Kubernetes cluster using kubeadm
context: You have been given access to three Ubuntu 22.04 nodes: master, worker1, and worker2. Your task is to set up a functional Kubernetes cluster.

tasks.title: Initialize the master node
tasks.description: Initialize a Kubernetes cluster on the master node using kubeadm with pod network CIDR 192.168.0.0/16

commands: kubectl get nodes --no-headers | grep master | grep Ready

hints: Don't forget to set up kubectl access after initialization
Check the kubeadm documentation for the exact syntax

tasks.title: Install CNI plugin
tasks.description: Install Calico CNI plugin to enable pod networking

commands: kubectl get pods -n calico-system --no-headers | grep Running | wc -l

hints: Apply the Calico manifest from the official documentation
Wait for all Calico pods to be in Running state
Use 'kubectl get pods -n calico-system' to check status

tasks.title: Join worker nodes
tasks.description: Join worker1 and worker2 nodes to the cluster

commands: kubectl get nodes --no-headers | grep Ready | wc -l

hints: Generate join command using 'kubeadm token create --print-join-command'
Execute the join command on each worker node
Verify all nodes show as Ready

tasks.title: Verify cluster health
tasks.description: Ensure all system pods are running and nodes are ready

commands: kubectl get pods -n kube-system --no-headers | grep -v Running | wc -l

hints: Check system pods with 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system'
All pods should be in Running or Completed state
Use 'kubectl describe pod' to troubleshoot any issues

resources.name: kubeconfig-sample
resources.content: # Sample kubeconfig will be generated after cluster init

solution: # Step 5: On worker nodes, run the join command
